---
title: "Quick Sort HTML Encoding"
output: github_document
---

# HTML Encoding Map

The Quick Sort page is encoded as one algorithm card with the standard viewer layout:

1. `\<title>` and the visible heading label the page "Quick Sort".
2. The top bar contains the project links and the complexity stat boxes.
3. `Problem Description` explains the three-way partition quick sort used in this repository.
4. `Code` is loaded from `main.js` into the shared `codeBox` element.
5. `Algorithm Steps` is a `\<ul>` whose entries all use `\<li><p>...\</p></li>` markup.
6. `Explanation` describes the pivot-based partitioning process, the average `O(n log n)` runtime, the worst-case `O(n^2)` runtime, and the extra arrays used by this implementation.
7. `Example/Demo` is stored in the final `exampleBox` preformatted block.

# Structural Notes

The wording is aligned to the actual JavaScript code, which partitions into `left`, `middle`, and `right` arrays and recursively chooses pivots for the subproblems. The HTML no longer relies on nested list markup inside the step list.
